failures.explanation: Missing activity chain for parameter(s): [FileList]. Requires insertion of 2 activities: [DatabricksNotebook, SetVariable]. Insert between: 'FTPLEPFile' → chain → 'Switch_ChkFiles'

The IfCondition activity "If_0A" is missing all branch-exclusive activities.
• Required TRUE branch activities (FTPLEPFile → ListFiles and their dependent SetVariable chain) are placed as root-level siblings that simply dependOn "If_0A" instead of being inside "ifTrueActivities".
• Required FALSE branch activities (Sqr2 → Sqr wait sequence) are also positioned at the root level and are not contained in "ifFalseActivities".
• Because branches were not embedded, the pipeline loses the implicit reconverge logic; the reconverge stage "ProcessNxtPgFiles" now depends on activities that should have been executed inside the IfCondition branches.
Expected: "If_0A" must carry two explicit branch lists:
   – ifTrueActivities: [FTPLEPFile, ListFiles_Routine, Set_FileList, ChkFiles_Switch, …] (complete chain until reconverge)
   – ifFalseActivities: [Sqr2, Sqr] (complete chain until reconverge)
Actual: "If_0A" declares an empty ifTrueActivities array and no ifFalseActivities array. All branch-specific logic sits outside the IfCondition, breaking logical isolation and reconverge behavior.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Open the "If_0A" activity and add two branch arrays: "ifTrueActivities" and "ifFalseActivities".
2. Move the following activities into "ifTrueActivities" preserving their existing dependsOn relationships:
   • "FTPLEPFile"
   • "ListFiles_Routine"
   • "Set_FileList"
   • "ChkFiles_Switch" and every activity that is reachable only through its success path up to but NOT including the reconverge stage "ProcessNxtPgFiles".
   Ensure internal dependsOn references remain unchanged after relocation.
3. Move the following activities into "ifFalseActivities" preserving their chain:
   • "Sqr2"
   • "Sqr"
   (Include their dependsOn settings so that "Sqr2" depends on "If_0A" and "Sqr" depends on "Sqr2".)
4. Remove any now-orphaned dependsOn references to "If_0A" from activities that have been relocated into the branches; branch placement automatically satisfies the dependency.
5. Place the reconverge wait activity "ProcessNxtPgFiles" (and any downstream chain) immediately after the "If_0A" activity at the root level so it executes once after either branch succeeds.
6. Validate that no activity outside "If_0A" directly depends on an activity residing inside one of the new branch lists; adjust dependsOn to point to "If_0A" or "ProcessNxtPgFiles" as appropriate.
7. Publish the pipeline and run validation to confirm that each branch executes exclusively and that the job reconverges exactly once at "ProcessNxtPgFiles".

failures.explanation:

• CurrDt: actual value "@pipeline().parameters.CurrDate" points to a pipeline parameter. The DataStage description "Variables.CurrDate" requires a stage-scoped variable reference.

Expected configuration:

Actual configuration:
  "RUNID": {"value": "@pipeline().parameters.RunID","type": "Expression"},
  "CurrDt": {"value": "@pipeline().parameters.CurrDate","type": "Expression"}

Parameter RUNID is mis-referenced.

• Variable "RunID" is not declared in properties.variables, so corrected reference would fail until declared.
All other parameters—including owner/secret pair (EAMRPTOwner / eamrpt_secret_name)—are correct and in proper order.

failures.explanation:
Stage "MbrDMHitList" in the DataStage job is of type "CWaitForFileActivity" and has two outgoing links ("HitListFound", "NoHitList"). Translation rules require such a stage to be converted to an IfCondition activity (because the job does not contain NestedIFChains). In the translated ADF pipeline the activity named "MbrDMHitList" is of type "Wait", which does not provide the required two-branch logic. As a result:
• No HitListFound / NoHitList branch evaluation occurs.
• Down-stream branching logic expected by stages "If_1C" and "Done" is lost.
Expected: an IfCondition activity whose expression tests the file-existence predicate and whose ifTrueActivities and ifFalseActivities represent the "HitListFound" and "NoHitList" branches, respectively. 
Actual: a Wait activity that unconditionally succeeds after one second, eliminating the branching requirement.

For parameter 'LinkCount' produced by in-built function 'JobLinkCount()':
• DatabricksNotebook 'Notebook_JobLinkCount_LinkCount' has an incorrect baseParameters structure.
  – Expected ONLY three parameters named exactly "JobLinkCount_a1", "JobLinkCount_a2", "JobLinkCount_a3" corresponding in order to the original arguments "ProcessCheckExtr_EE.':Initial.JobName", "Trans1", "Output".
  – An additional Owner parameter was not required, yet the notebook contains an "IDSOwner" parameter followed by an unrelated "GlobalEnvironment" parameter.
  – The required parameter-naming convention <FunctionName>_a<N> is not followed: the notebook uses "JobName_a1–a4" instead of "JobLinkCount_a<N>".
  – Owner-secret pairing rule is violated: because an "IDSOwner" parameter was mistakenly included, its mandatory secret companion "ids_secret_name" is also missing.
• As a result the notebook cannot resolve its argument list and will fail at runtime.

For parameter 'RunCycle' produced by in-built function 'OpenRunCycle()':
• DatabricksNotebook 'Notebook_OpenRunCycle_Cycle' has an incorrect baseParameters structure.
  – Expected parameter names: "OpenRunCycle_a1" … "OpenRunCycle_a5" strictly following argument order ($IDSOwner, Initial.Source, Initial.Target, Initial.Subject, Initial.Frequency).
  – The notebook instead uses descriptive names ("IDSOwner", "Source", "Target", "Subject", "Frequency").
  – An owner parameter "$IDSOwner" is passed but its mandatory secret companion "ids_secret_name" is missing immediately after it, violating the Owner-Secret Pairing rule.
  – Because of the wrong names the execution notebook cannot deserialize the arguments correctly.

The DataStage stage "If_8D" has two independent outgoing conditional links:
 • Link "Run" → stage "LoadMbrRiskMesr" executes when "LoadMbrRiskMesr" is NOT found in the ExclusionList.
 • Link "Run2" → stage "LoadKMbrRiskMesr" executes when "LoadKMbrRiskMesr" is NOT found in the ExclusionList.
This allows either notebook to run independently.

In the translated ADF pipeline the single activity "If_8D" uses the expression:
  @and(equals(contains(split(string(pipeline().parameters.ExclusionList), ';'), 'LoadMbrRiskMesr'), false),
       equals(contains(split(string(pipeline().parameters.ExclusionList), ';'), 'LoadKMbrRiskMesr'), false))
This expression evaluates to true ONLY when BOTH items are absent from the list. As a result, when exactly one of the two loads is excluded the other one will also be suppressed – behaviour that diverges from the original job.

Expected:
 • Two separate conditional evaluations, one for each notebook, so that each load may run independently.
 • Each notebook should be placed inside its own IfCondition.ifTrueActivities.

Actual:
 • One IfCondition governs both notebooks simultaneously, forcing an unintended logical AND between the two predicates.

The activity "IdsDmMbrshDmMbrLmtExtr" passes the parameter "IDSRunCycle" with the expression "@pipeline().parameters.IDSRunCycle", but the pipeline-level parameter "IDSRunCycle" is not declared in the root-level "properties.parameters" section of the ADF pipeline JSON. According to the parameter-translation rules, every parameter referenced through "@pipeline().parameters.<name>" must have a corresponding declaration. The absence of this declaration will cause runtime failures because ADF cannot resolve an undefined pipeline parameter.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Open the root of the ADF pipeline JSON and locate the "properties.parameters" object.
2. Insert the following key-value pair (order within the object is not important, but ensure the full object syntax is respected):
   "IDSRunCycle": {
     "type": "String",
     "defaultValue": ""
   }
3. Save and publish the pipeline. This addition registers "IDSRunCycle" so that the expression "@pipeline().parameters.IDSRunCycle" used inside the "IdsDmMbrshDmMbrLmtExtr" ExecutePipeline activity can resolve correctly.
4. No other changes are required for this activity; its internal "parameters" block is already correct.

    "pSource": "DATE",
    "pSourceFormat": "CURRENT",
    "pOutFormat": "SYBTIMESTAMP"

    "pSource": "DATE",
    "pSourceFormat": "CURRENT",
    "pOutFormat": "SYBTIMESTAMP"
  }
failures.suggested_fix:

   }

failures.explanation:
The Switch activity "Switch_If_01A" does not faithfully implement the DataStage stage "If_01A". Issues detected:
• Incorrect typeProperties.on.value:
  – Expected expression to evaluate both the ancestor branch-flag variable (var_CountChk_branch = '0') AND the original detectionCondition "Count(Variables.ExclusionList, ';':'UwsIdsPpbmGrpXrefExtr':'';') = 0".
  – Actual expression is "@if(and(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'), true()), 1, 0)", which omits the detectionCondition completely and uses a placeholder "true()".
• Missing incorporation of nested and()/or() calls to split the logical tests as required.
• The expression does not reference variables("var_If_01A_branch") anywhere outside the SetVariable activity, potentially causing downstream gating failures.
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify without renaming any activity:
1. Open "Switch_If_01A" > typeProperties.on.value and replace the current value with the EXACT expression below (no extra spaces):
   "@if(and(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'), equals(count(split(variables('ExclusionList'),';:UwsIdsPpbmGrpXrefExtr:;')),0)),1,0)"
   – The and() call now contains exactly two arguments as mandated.
   – The first argument enforces the ancestor branch-flag (CountChk False branch).
   – The second argument implements the detectionCondition logic using ADF functions.
2. Ensure there is only one and() wrapper; do NOT nest additional and() / or() beyond two arguments per call.
3. Validate that the SetVariable "Set_var_If_01A_True" and "Set_var_If_01A_False" remain unchanged.
4. Publish pipeline changes and re-test execution.

failures.explanation:
The Switch activity "Switch_If_0B" is an incorrect translation of DataStage stage "If_0B".
• Missing detectionCondition in typeProperties.on.value. The required logical test "Count(Variables.ExclusionList, ';':'UwsIdsPpbmGrpXrefLoad':'';') = 0" is absent.
• Expression erroneously duplicates the logic used for "If_01A" and again uses "true()" as a placeholder.
• The Switch does not evaluate any branch-flag from its own parent (var_If_01A_branch) which is needed to avoid unintended execution when the parent branch is false.
failures.suggested_fix:
Apply the following corrections:
1. Edit "Switch_If_0B" > typeProperties.on.value. Replace with:
   "@if(and(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'), and(equals(variables('var_If_01A_branch'),'1'), equals(count(split(variables('ExclusionList'),';:UwsIdsPpbmGrpXrefLoad:;')),0))),1,0)"
   – Outer and() has exactly 2 arguments: ancestor CountChk flag and nested and().
   – Inner and() has exactly 2 arguments: parent branch flag var_If_01A_branch and the detectionCondition.
2. Confirm no additional arguments are appended to any and()/or() call.
3. Keep SetVariable activities intact.
4. Save and publish.

failures.explanation:
Switch "Switch_If_0B_Continuation" fails to honour the rule for "Parent Switch Branch Flag" detectionCondition.
• Current expression "@if(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'),1,0)" checks only var_CountChk_branch and ignores the immediate parent branch flag var_If_0B_branch.
• Omitting this flag allows the continuation branch to run even when "If_0B" evaluated to FALSE, breaking branch isolation.
failures.suggested_fix:
Correct the on.value expression as follows:
1. Navigate to "Switch_If_0B_Continuation" > typeProperties.on.value.
2. Replace the value with:
   "@if(and(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'), equals(variables('var_If_0B_branch'),'1')),1,0)"
   – First argument ensures CountChk False branch.
   – Second argument enforces parent branch flag var_If_0B_branch = '1'.
3. Verify there are exactly two arguments in the outer and() call.
4. Retain existing SetVariable activities.
5. Publish and validate the pipeline execution.

To remediate without renaming activities:
1. Open the 'WriteRunID' DatabricksNotebook activity.
2. Locate properties.typeProperties.baseParameters.
3. Replace the existing EntryName definition with EXACTLY:
   "EntryName": {
     "value": "@concat(variables('JobName'),':RunID')",
     "type": "Expression"
   }
4. Ensure no additional spaces or characters are present.
5. Save and publish the pipeline.
This correction applies the SpecialCase rule by appending the literal ':RunID' directly after the JobName variable and keeps parameter ordering intact.

failures.explanation:
The Switch activity "CountChk_Switch" is intended to translate stage "CountChk" whose detectionCondition is "LinkCount.LinkCount <> 0".  
• The current on.value expression is "@if(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'), '1'),1,0)" which merely evaluates an internal branch-flag variable and does NOT evaluate the actual detection condition from the DataStage job.  
• Because the detection condition is omitted, the pipeline cannot decide the correct branch at run-time, breaking logical parity with the source job.  
• Expected: on.value must follow the required pattern "@if(<condition>, 1, 0)" where <condition> is the logical result of "LinkCount.LinkCount <> 0" (True branch) with no additional gating variables for this root switch.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Open the "CountChk_Switch" activity in the ADF designer (JSON view).  
2. Replace the entire typeProperties.on block with:  
   "on": {
     "value": "@if(not(equals(variables('LinkCount'),'0')),1,0)",
     "type": "Expression"
   }  
   – The expression converts the logical test "LinkCount.LinkCount <> 0" into ADF syntax.  
3. Do NOT alter the cases/defaultActivities arrays.  
4. Publish the pipeline and test: when LinkCount ≠ 0, the True case executes; otherwise the default case executes.

failures.explanation:
The Switch activity "If_3A_Switch" translates stage "If_3A" which has detectionCondition "Count (Variables.ExclusionList, \";\": \"WebUserInfoAHYEvtSchedulingExtrSeq\" :\";\") = 0" and is gated by CountChk’s FalseBranch.  
• Current on.value expression: "@if(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'),1,0)".  
  – It checks only the gating variable from the parent switch and completely omits evaluation of the detection condition itself.  
• Required logic: on.value must be the AND of the parent-branch flag AND the detection condition.  
• Missing detection condition causes incorrect branch selection and may execute downstream activities even when exclusion list indicates the sequence should be skipped.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Edit "If_3A_Switch" → typeProperties.on.  
2. Replace with:  
   "on": {
     "value": "@if(and(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'),
                     equals(count(split(variables('ExclusionList'),';'), 'WebUserInfoAHYEvtSchedulingExtrSeq'), 0)),
                 1,
                 0)",
     "type": "Expression"
   }  
   – The first argument enforces parent gating (var_CountChk_branch = '0').  
   – The second argument replicates the detection condition using ADF functions (count of token = 0).  
3. Leave cases/defaultActivities unchanged.  
4. Save and publish.  Verify that the True branch only runs when both conditions are satisfied.

failures.explanation:
The Switch "If_3B_Switch" represents stage "If_3B" with detectionCondition "Count (Variables.ExclusionList, \";\": \"WebUserInfoAHYEvtSchedulingLoadSeq\" :\";\") = 0" gated by CountChk FalseBranch AND If_3A TrueBranch.  
• Current on.value: "@if(and(and(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'), '0'), equals(variables('var_If_3A_branch'), '1')), true()), 1, 0)".  
  – Parent-branch flags are present but the actual detection condition (token count check) is missing, replaced by the placeholder true().  
• As a result, the switch always evaluates to true whenever the gating flags match, ignoring the exclusion-list logic, diverging from DataStage behaviour.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Open "If_3B_Switch" JSON and replace the on expression with:  
   "on": {
     "value": "@if(and(and(equals(variables('var_CountChk_branch'),'0'),
                           equals(variables('var_If_3A_branch'),'1')),
                     equals(count(split(variables('ExclusionList'),';'), 'WebUserInfoAHYEvtSchedulingLoadSeq'), 0)),
                 1,
                 0)",
     "type": "Expression"
   }  
   – This expression retains both parent gate checks and adds the correct exclusion-list comparison.  
2. No other parts of the switch require modification.  
3. Publish and validate: the True branch now fires only when all gating flags are satisfied AND the exclusion-list condition is true.

failures.explanation:
The DatabricksNotebook activity that represents the routine stage "WriteRunID" has multiple parameter issues:

failures.explanation:

This violates correct branch mapping rules:
• A single IfCondition must encapsulate all mutually-exclusive branches of its source CCondition stage.
• Each branch-exclusive activity must be placed inside the proper branch list of that one IfCondition.
• After the branches, a single reconverge activity (Wait) must appear once at the root level.
Expected: one activity named "If_3D" with its ifTrueActivities containing BOTH "LoadClmLnProcCdMod" and "K_CLM_LN_PROC_CD_MOD", and its ifFalseActivities containing a single Wait activity. Actual: two separate IfConditions, causing loss of mutual exclusion semantics and duplicated evaluation of the same predicate.

The DataStage stage "If_3D" does not appear as an activity with the same logical identity in the translated pipeline. Instead, two newly-named IfCondition activities were introduced, leaving the original stage effectively untranslated. This breaches the requirement that every input stage (except explicitly exempted categories) be represented once in the ADF pipeline.

failures.explanation: Input stage "Exec_Invc_DT_File_Cnt" of type "CExecCommandActivity" (Path = ['echo ']) is completely absent in the translated ADF pipeline. Because the Path value is not exactly ['echo'] (it has a trailing space), this stage is NOT exempt from translation. Expected a corresponding DatabricksNotebook activity that executes the underlying echo command (or an equivalent lightweight notebook), positioned between translated activities "Any8" and "Exec_INVC_CNT_VAL". Its absence breaks the original execution flow and causes downstream variables (INVC_DT, BILL_START_DT, BILL_END_DT, FILE_CNT, LOB) to be unresolved.

The DataStage CCondition stage "If_5A" has two branches (Run / Skip). The Run branch exclusively contains stage "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq" before the flow reconverges at sequencer "Or_5A". 

Expected translation:
• Activity "If_5A" of type "IfCondition" with its Run-branch logic placed inside "ifTrueActivities" list.
• "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq" (type ExecutePipeline) MUST be the first element of "ifTrueActivities" so it executes only when the Run predicate evaluates to true.
• The Skip branch should contain no activities (or a Wait) inside "ifFalseActivities".
• The reconverge activity (logical equivalent to sequencer "Or_5A") must be placed once, OUTSIDE the IfCondition activity, as a root-level sibling so both branches converge correctly.

Actual translation:
• "If_5A" is created, but both "ifTrueActivities" and "ifFalseActivities" are empty arrays.
• "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq" is placed at the root level and merely dependsOn the success of "If_5A". This causes the activity to run even when the Skip branch should have been chosen, violating branch isolation rules.
• A Wait activity "Or_5A" is placed at root level, but because the branches were never defined inside the IfCondition, real convergence semantics are broken.

Expected vs Actual summary:
  - Placement of "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq": expected inside IfCondition.ifTrueActivities, found at root level.
  - IfCondition.ifTrueActivities and ifFalseActivities: expected populated, found empty.
  - Reconverge activity location: expected outside and executed after both branches, current Wait activity does not truly represent branch convergence because branches were not defined.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Modify activity "If_5A" (do NOT rename):
   • Move the existing empty array in "typeProperties.ifTrueActivities" to contain one element – a reference to activity "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq" as JSON object:
     "ifTrueActivities": [ {
         "name": "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq",
         "type": "ExecutePipeline",
         "dependsOn": [],
         "policy": { "secureInput": false },
         "typeProperties": { /* keep current contents unchanged */ }
       } ]
   • Leave "ifFalseActivities": [] (or add a single 1-second Wait if a physical activity is required by your standards).
   • Remove any "dependsOn" array from the newly embedded "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq" because it now inherits branch execution context.

2. Delete the root-level "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq" activity object that currently sits outside the IfCondition.

3. Adjust the Wait activity "Or_5A":
   • Keep it at root level but change its "dependsOn" so it references the branch activities INSIDE the IfCondition, not the IfCondition itself. Example:
       "dependsOn": [
         { "activity": "If_5A", "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"] }
       ]
     (Because the IfCondition now truly governs branch completion, waiting on the IfCondition is sufficient.)

4. Validate pipeline:
   • Confirm that executing the pipeline with condition evaluating to false skips execution of "IdsWebDMDrugPrereqSeq".
   • Confirm success path still converges correctly at "Or_5A".

Repeat the above procedure for every other IfCondition activity (If_5B, If_5C, If_5D, If_5E, If_6A, If_4A) to ensure all branch-exclusive stages are embedded within their corresponding IfCondition.ifTrueActivities or ifFalseActivities lists and reconverge outside the IfCondition. Secret parameters and existing notebook/ExecutePipeline configurations remain unchanged during the move.

Parameter validation failure detected in activity 'DatabricksNotebook_WriteRunID'.  
• Parameter involved: EntryName  
• Nature of failure: The Input DataStage stage 'WriteRunID' defines parameter "EntryName" with Description "Initial_JobName:\"RunID\"", indicating a concatenation of the pipeline parameter Initial_JobName and literal "RunID".  
  – Expected ADF passing rule: Non-literal component (Initial_JobName) must be referenced via expression {"value": "@pipeline().parameters.Initial_JobName", "type": "Expression"}. Literal component (":RunID") must be appended using concat.  
  – Expected final ADF value structure:  
    {
      "value": "@concat(pipeline().parameters.Initial_JobName,'RunID')",
      "type": "Expression"
    }  
• Actual state: The translated ADF activity passes EntryName as plain literal string "RunID" (no expression object, no pipeline parameter reference, no concat).  
• Consequence: Incorrect value is delivered to the notebook, breaking expected functionality.
failures.suggested_fix:

2. Delete the existing key–value pair:

3. Insert the corrected parameter immediately at the same position:

     "value": "@concat(pipeline().parameters.Initial_JobName,'RunID')",
     "type": "Expression"
   }  
4. Verify no duplicate "EntryName" keys remain.  
5. Publish pipeline changes and re-validate to ensure the parameter now resolves to the concatenation of pipeline parameter Initial_JobName and literal "RunID" during execution.

failures.explanation:
The translated Switch activity "If_1A" has an invalid dependency configuration.
• Issue: The property "dependsOn.activity" is set to "Set_FileList", which is an activity located INSIDE the "defaultActivities" branch of the upstream Switch "CountChk".
• Rule Violation 1 (Check-8 #4): For NestedIFChains, each Switch must depend on the stage listed in the chain’s "dependsOn" field ("CountChk" for "If_1A") or the immediately preceding root-level Switch—not on an internal branch activity.
• Rule Violation 2 (Check-8 #5): A Switch’s "dependsOn.activity" MUST NOT reference an activity that resides inside any Switch branch; "Set_FileList" is inside the default branch of "CountChk".
• Expected State: "If_1A" should have exactly one entry in its dependsOn array with "activity": "CountChk" and "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"].
• Actual State: "If_1A" depends on "Set_FileList", causing inappropriate coupling to a branch-internal activity and breaking branch-flag gating integrity.
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify the existing "If_1A" Switch activity WITHOUT renaming it.
1. Remove the current dependsOn object that lists "Set_FileList".
2. Add the correct dependsOn definition:
   "dependsOn": [
     {
       "activity": "CountChk",
       "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"]
     }
   ]
3. Ensure no other dependsOn entries remain.
4. Validate that all internal branch activities of "If_1A" remain unchanged.
5. Publish the pipeline and re-test to confirm that "If_1A" now triggers after the root-level "CountChk" Switch completes, satisfying Check-8 rule requirements.

     }

Expected state: An IfCondition activity that evaluates the presence of the required file and, based on the predicate result, routes execution to the correct branch (success branch → logic originally driven by "next"; failure branch → logic originally driven by "FileNotFound").

failures.explanation: Stage "Wait_For_FileClnclCls" is a CWaitForFileActivity that contains two OutputPins ("next" and "NoFile") and therefore requires conditional branching in ADF. The translated pipeline represents this stage as a simple Wait activity that always continues on Succeeded and sends control to the Email notification path. This translation drops the branch that should execute when the file exists, mis-routes the "file not found" logic, and provides no mechanism to evaluate the file presence condition. Expected: an IfCondition activity named "Wait_For_FileClnclCls" whose expression tests for the file’s existence, with its ifTrueActivities branch containing the logic for the "next" link (Wait_For_File_Cond) and its ifFalseActivities branch containing the logic for the "NoFile" link (Email2DAT → Terminator1). Actual: a Wait activity with unconditional success continuation.

Stage "Wait_For_File_Cond" is a CWaitForFileActivity with two OutputPins ("next1" for success and "FileNotFound" for failure). In the translated pipeline it appears as a Wait activity that unconditionally triggers its successors, eliminating the conditional branching required to route to "Wait_For_ModelOutput" on success and to "Email3DAT" → "Terminator2" on failure. Expected translation is an IfCondition activity that evaluates the file existence at the configured path and directs control to the correct branch. Actual translation is a Wait activity that cannot perform this logic, resulting in loss of functional parity.

Stage "Wait_For_ModelOutput" is a CWaitForFileActivity possessing two OutputPins ("next4" for success and "FileNotFound" for failure). The ADF translation represents it as a Wait activity that always reports success, thereby discarding the required decision logic that should distinguish between the two outputs. As a consequence, the email-failure path (Email4DAT → Terminator3) is never reached and normal processing (Any4 → Any5 → WriteRunID) cannot be gated properly. Expected: an IfCondition that evaluates file existence and directs flow accordingly. Actual: simple Wait activity without conditional evaluation.

failures.explanation: Stage "If_Comsn" in the DataStage job has a single "Process" output that drives stage "If_4A" until control reconverges at sequencer "Or_4A". In the translated ADF pipeline the corresponding IfCondition activity "If_Comsn" has empty ifTrueActivities/ifFalseActivities arrays, while the branch-specific activity "If_4A" (and all its downstream activities) are placed outside the IfCondition. Expected: every activity that belongs exclusively to the true branch must reside inside IfCondition.ifTrueActivities, the false branch may be empty, and the reconverge activity must appear once outside the IfCondition. Actual: branch activities are incorrectly located at the root level and linked through dependsOn, breaking logical branch isolation.
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify WITHOUT renaming activities:
1. Cut activity "If_4A" and all activities downstream up to the reconverge activity "Or_4A" and paste them inside the ifTrueActivities array of "If_Comsn" maintaining their internal dependency chain.
2. Remove the current dependsOn entry from "If_4A" that points to "If_Comsn"; inside the true branch no dependsOn is required for the first activity.
3. Ensure the last activity inside the true branch (currently "Or_4A") is removed from the true branch list and placed immediately AFTER "If_Comsn" at the root level. Set its dependsOn so that it waits on both the true branch’s terminal activity and the IfCondition skip path.
4. Leave ifFalseActivities empty (skip branch) because the original DataStage stage has no explicit skip logic.
5. Validate that no other root-level activities still depend directly on "If_Comsn" except the reconverge activity.

failures.explanation: DataStage stage "If_4A" has a "Run" branch leading to "ConcatComsnTrans" and a "Skip" branch leading directly to sequencer "Or_4A". In the translated pipeline the IfCondition activity "If_4A" contains no activities in either ifTrueActivities or ifFalseActivities. "ConcatComsnTrans" is outside the IfCondition and linked via dependsOn. This violates branch containment rules: branch-exclusive activities must be nested inside the correct branch list.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Move activity "ConcatComsnTrans" inside the ifTrueActivities array of "If_4A" and adjust its dependsOn so it has none (first item in branch).
2. Create a Wait activity named "Skip_If_4A" of one-second duration inside ifFalseActivities to mirror the Skip path (optional but recommended for symmetry).
3. Remove "ConcatComsnTrans" from the root-level activities list.
4. Keep "Or_4A" at the root level; modify its dependsOn so it waits on both the last true-branch activity ("ConcatComsnTrans") and "Skip_If_4A".
5. Confirm there are no remaining direct dependsOn links from "ConcatComsnTrans" to "If_4A" outside the branch.

failures.explanation: Stage "If_4B" in DataStage directs the "Run" branch to job "IdsComsnTransFkey" and the "Skip" branch to sequencer "Or_4B". In ADF, activity "If_4B" has empty branch arrays while "IdsComsnTransFkey" is placed outside. Expected branch nesting is absent, breaking logical flow.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Relocate activity "IdsComsnTransFkey" into the ifTrueActivities array of "If_4B" with no dependsOn.
2. Insert a one-second Wait activity "Skip_If_4B" into ifFalseActivities.
3. Remove root-level instance of "IdsComsnTransFkey".
4. Ensure reconverge Wait "Or_4B" remains root-level and depends on both "IdsComsnTransFkey" (true branch) and "Skip_If_4B" (false branch).
5. Eliminate any obsolete dependsOn links from "IdsComsnTransFkey" to "If_4B" outside the branch.

failures.explanation: DataStage stage "If_4C" controls a true branch to Unix command "RmComsnTrans" and a skip branch to sequencer "Or_4C". Translated IfCondition "If_4C" has no nested activities; "RmComsnTrans" sits outside, violating branch encapsulation.
failures.suggested_fix:

• The reconverge stage "Or_2A" must sit exactly once at root level and depend on the nested chain, not on activities that will be relocated.

failures.suggested_fix:

2. Remove the root-level definitions of "If_2A", "LoadClmB_JRNL_ENTRY_TRANS", and "PsiIdsJrnlEntryTransClmBalSeq" after they have been moved, so they do not appear twice.
3. Update the root-level Wait activity "Or_2A":

     ‑ "If_Clm" (to capture the False branch) with condition "Succeeded".
4. Verify no orphaned dependencies remain in any other activity.
5. Validate that the relocated chain executes in order and that "Or_2A" is the single reconverge point outside "If_Clm".

Actual:

  - The secret parameter is positioned after arguments a2–a5, breaking the owner-secret adjacency requirement.
  - Because the secret is misplaced, any downstream notebook expecting the secret directly after its owner will fail to resolve the credential at runtime.
Expected configuration (parameter order excerpt):
  "baseParameters": {
    "OpenRunCycle_a1": {"value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSOwner", "type": "Expression"},
    "ids_secret_name": {"value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSSecret", "type": "Expression"},
    "OpenRunCycle_a2": {"value": "@variables('Source')", "type": "Expression"},
    ... }
Actual configuration (parameter order excerpt):
  "OpenRunCycle_a1" → "OpenRunCycle_a2" → "OpenRunCycle_a3" → "OpenRunCycle_a4" → "OpenRunCycle_a5" → "IDSOwner" → "ids_secret_name"

1. Open the 'baseParameters' section of activity 'OpenRunCycle'.
2. Cut the entire key–value pair for "ids_secret_name" currently located after "IDSOwner".
3. Paste that key–value pair immediately after "OpenRunCycle_a1" so that it becomes the second entry in the object.
   Correct snippet:
   "OpenRunCycle_a1": {
       "value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSOwner",
       "type": "Expression"
   },
   "ids_secret_name": {
       "value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSSecret",
       "type": "Expression"
   },
4. Confirm no other keys exist between the owner parameter and its secret.
5. Save and validate the pipeline. Secret parameters MUST be placed directly after their owner parameter to comply with translation guidelines.

failures.explanation: The DataStage condition stage "If_1A" determines whether to execute job "FctsIdsMbrshpBal1Seq" by evaluating the expression "Count (Variables.ExclusionList, \";\": \"FctsIdsMbrshpBal1Seq\" :\";\") = 0".  In the translated ADF pipeline, activity "If_1A" uses the hard-coded expression "@equals(0,0)", which always returns TRUE.  This eliminates the required branching logic and causes the pipeline to execute the child sequence unconditionally.  Additionally, because the false branch is never reachable, the downstream reconverge activity "Or_1A" may receive incomplete branch predecessors, breaking logical parity with the original job.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Open activity "If_1A" in the ADF pipeline editor.
2. Replace typeProperties.expression.value with the correct dynamic expression:
   "@equals(count(split(variables('ExclusionList'), ';FctsIdsMbrshpBal1Seq;')),1)"
   (This evaluates to TRUE only when the job name is not found in the exclusion list.)
3. Ensure typeProperties.ifTrueActivities contains only "FctsIdsMbrshpBal1Seq".
4. Leave typeProperties.ifFalseActivities empty; keep root-level Wait "Or_1A" outside the IfCondition exactly as downstream reconverge.
5. Validate and publish the pipeline so that the IfCondition now mirrors the original DataStage decision logic.

failures.explanation: DataStage stage "If_1B" evaluates "Count (Variables.ExclusionList, \";\": \"FctsIdsMbrshpBal2Seq\" :\";\") = 0" to decide whether to call child sequence "FctsIdsMbrshpBal2Seq".  The translated ADF activity "If_1B" again uses the placeholder expression "@equals(0,0)", always yielding TRUE and ignoring the exclusion list.  Consequently, the skip path leading to sequencer "Or_1B" is never honoured, diverging from the original control flow.
failures.suggested_fix:

4. Confirm that downstream Wait "Or_1B" remains a root-level sibling depending on both "If_1B" and "FctsIdsMbrshpBal2Seq".

failures.explanation:
The Switch activity translated from the DataStage stage "If_2A_Continuation" contains an incorrect gating expression in typeProperties.on.value. 
• Expected logic: the Switch must fire ONLY when the parent-branch flag variable var_If_2A_branch_True equals '1'. Therefore the expression must be in the canonical form @if(equals(variables('var_If_2A_branch_True'),'1'),1,0).
• Actual logic: @if(or(equals(variables('var_If_2A_branch_True'),'1'),equals(variables('var_If_2A_branch_False'),'0')),1,0) makes the condition always evaluate to 1 because exactly one of the two variables will always satisfy its equality test. This effectively removes the intended gating logic and violates the specification for "Parent Switch Branch Flag" handling.
• Impact: The True branch of this Switch will always execute, regardless of the actual outcome of the parent Switch "If_2A", breaking branch isolation and potentially causing incorrect downstream processing.
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify the existing Switch activity "If_2A_Continuation" WITHOUT renaming it:
1. Open the activity properties and replace the entire content of typeProperties.on.value with:
   @if(equals(variables('var_If_2A_branch_True'),'1'),1,0)
2. Do NOT reference var_If_2A_branch_False in this expression.
3. Ensure the expression string is wrapped exactly as shown, including the outer @if() call returning 1 or 0.
4. Leave all dependsOn, cases, defaultActivities and internal activity definitions unchanged.
5. Validate the pipeline to confirm that the Switch now triggers only when var_If_2A_branch_True equals '1'.

failures.explanation:
DatabricksNotebook activity "MvLoadFile" does not comply with Generic_ADLS_File_Operations parameter requirements.
• command parameter value is "mv -f AccumReconExtr.dat processed/AccumReconExtr.dat.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S); compress -f #$FilePath#/load/processed/AccumReconExtr.dat*" – expected one of: copy | delete | move | touch | cat.
• src_file_with_path is " " (blank) – for move operations this must be a fully-qualified source file path ending with the file name.
• trgt_file_with_path is " " (blank) – for move operations this must be a fully-qualified target file path.
• cut_column_index is present with value " " – optional but harmless.
Expected configuration:
  "command": "move",
  "src_file_with_path": "@pipeline().parameters.<SrcFilePathParam>",
  "trgt_file_with_path": "@pipeline().parameters.<TrgtFilePathParam>".
Actual configuration contains an invalid free-form shell string and missing required paths.
failures.suggested_fix:

1. Open the baseParameters object of "MvLoadFile".
2. Replace the command key:
   "command": "move"
3. Insert correct source path expression:
   "src_file_with_path": {
       "value": "@pipeline().parameters.FilePath + '/load/AccumReconExtr.dat'",
       "type": "Expression"
   }
4. Insert correct target path expression:
   "trgt_file_with_path": {
       "value": "@pipeline().parameters.FilePath + '/load/processed/AccumReconExtr.dat.' + formatDateTime(utcNow(),'yyyyMMddHHmmss')",
       "type": "Expression"
   }

failures.explanation: The DataStage sequencer stage "Or_1A" is the reconverge point for the Skip and Load branches coming from "If_1A". In the translated ADF pipeline, the Wait activity "Or_1A" has two dependency entries: one for "If_1A" and one for the notebook activity "IdsMbrAuditZeroExtr" with dependencyConditions set to ["Succeeded"]. When the Skip branch is taken, "IdsMbrAuditZeroExtr" is not executed and ends in a "Skipped" state. Because "Or_1A" waits specifically for "Succeeded" on that activity, the reconverge never occurs, breaking the control flow. Expected: "Or_1A" should either depend solely on "If_1A" ("Succeeded") or, if it also references the notebook activity, include both "Succeeded" and "Skipped" dependencyConditions so the reconverge works for either branch. Actual: the activity depends on a success that never happens in the Skip scenario.
failures.suggested_fix:
Update activity "Or_1A" as follows:
1. Remove the entire dependsOn object that references "IdsMbrAuditZeroExtr".
2. Keep only the dependsOn object that references "If_1A" with "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"].
3. Ensure no other dependsOn entries exist.
4. Validate by running a test where the Skip branch is taken; confirm "Or_1A" now executes immediately after "If_1A" succeeds.

failures.explanation:
CUserVarsActivity "Variables" contains four inbuilt functions (GetExclusionList, GetFkeyCodes, FORMAT.DATE, GetRunInfo). Translation collapses them into a single DatabricksNotebook "Variables_routines" and four subsequent SetVariable activities, violating required one-function-one-notebook-one-setvariable pattern and proper parameter naming rules.
Additional issues:
• Notebook path points to pseudo notebook "/_chained" instead of individual routine paths.
• baseParameters use custom keys (routineList, JobNameParam, SourceParam, GlobalEnvironment) instead of positional keys <FunctionName>_a1 ...
• Secret parameter "ids_secret_name" missing for owner argument in GetExclusionList.
• DependsOn chain does not alternate Notebook → SetVariable per function; multiple SetVariable activities depend on a single notebook.
failures.suggested_fix:
Repurpose WITHOUT renaming:
1. Delete DatabricksNotebook "Variables_routines" and the four SetVariable activities.
2. Create the following eight activities chained sequentially after "ReadExclusionListFile" (or its replacement upstream):
   a. DatabricksNotebook "GetExclusionList_nb" with notebookPath "@concat(pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_routine_notebook_path, '/sequencer_routines/GetExclusionList')" and baseParameters:
      "GetExclusionList_a1": {"value": "@variables('JobName')","type":"Expression"}
   b. SetVariable "SetVariable_ExclusionList" dependsOn "GetExclusionList_nb" and sets variable "ExclusionList" to "@string(activity('GetExclusionList_nb').output.runOutput)".
   c. DatabricksNotebook "GetFkeyCodes_nb" dependsOn "SetVariable_ExclusionList" with notebookPath for GetFkeyCodes and baseParameters:
      "GetFkeyCodes_a1":"IDS", "GetFkeyCodes_a2":"1", "GetFkeyCodes_a3":"SOURCE SYSTEM", "GetFkeyCodes_a4":{ "value":"@variables('Source')","type":"Expression"}, "GetFkeyCodes_a5":"X".
   d. SetVariable "SetVariable_SrcSysCdSk" dependsOn "GetFkeyCodes_nb" and assigns variable "SrcSysCdSk".
   e. DatabricksNotebook "FORMAT_DATE_nb" dependsOn "SetVariable_SrcSysCdSk" and uses special baseParameters {"pInDate":"string('@DATE')","pSource":"DATE","pSourceFormat":"CURRENT","pOutFormat":"CCYY-MM-DD"}.
   f. SetVariable "SetVariable_CurrentDate" dependsOn "FORMAT_DATE_nb" and assigns variable "CurrentDate".
   g. DatabricksNotebook "GetRunInfo_nb" dependsOn "SetVariable_CurrentDate" with notebookPath for GetRunInfo and baseParameters:
      "GetRunInfo_a1": {"value": "@variables('JobName')","type":"Expression"}
   h. SetVariable "SetVariable_RunID" dependsOn "GetRunInfo_nb" and assigns variable "RunID".
3. Ensure each Notebook → SetVariable pair links with dependsOn.dependencyConditions ["Succeeded"].
4. Declare secret parameters where owner parameters are used; for GetExclusionList no owner parameter exists so no secret required.
5. Remove any obsolete references to deleted activities from downstream dependsOn chains.

failures.explanation: Stage "Wait_For_F_HitList_File" in the DataStage job is of type "CWaitForFileActivity" and has two outgoing links ("File" and "No_File"). According to translation rules when no NestedIFChains block is present, every such stage MUST be translated into an "IfCondition" activity so that the two possible outcomes are represented as true/false branches. In the translated ADF pipeline the corresponding activity "Wait_For_F_HitList_File" is of type "Wait". This activity type cannot model the two-branch logic required for the original stage and therefore the translation is not faithful. Expected: an "IfCondition" activity with an expression evaluating the file-presence predicate, containing branch activities for the "File" case (true) and leaving the false branch empty, followed by a reconverge activity. Actual: a single "Wait" activity with no branching logic.

Activity "ProcessCheckExtr" is the translation of DataStage stage "ProcessCheckExtr" that contains the owner parameter "$IDSOwner". The corresponding baseParameters block in the DatabricksNotebook activity passes the key "IDSOwner" correctly but omits the mandatory secret parameter "ids_secret_name" that must immediately follow the owner parameter. This violates the owner-secret pairing rule and the required parameter order rule. Expected:
  "IDSOwner": {"value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSOwner","type": "Expression"},
  "ids_secret_name": {"value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSSecret","type": "Expression"}
Actual:
  "IDSOwner" is present but "ids_secret_name" is missing.
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify the DatabricksNotebook activity "ProcessCheckExtr" without changing its name.
1. Open the activity's typeProperties.baseParameters section.
2. Immediately AFTER the existing "IDSOwner" entry, insert the following key-value pair:
   "ids_secret_name": {
     "value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSSecret",
     "type": "Expression"
   }
3. Ensure the resulting order of the first two parameters is exactly:
   a. IDSOwner
   b. ids_secret_name
4. Validate that no duplicate "ids_secret_name" exists elsewhere in the same baseParameters object.
5. Publish the pipeline so that the secret parameter is available at runtime.

Secret parameters MUST be placed directly after their owner parameter.

Since the DataStage Name is "Arg10", the translated key must be "Arg10".
• Owner-secret pairing violation – because "Arg10" (which carries the owner value) is missing, the accompanying secret parameter must be associated with "Arg10". Although "ids_secret_name" exists, its placement must be immediately after the corrected owner parameter.
• Extraneous parameter "GlobalEnvironment" has been added. This parameter does not exist in the source stage and must be removed to maintain one-to-one parity.

1. Insert the missing owner parameter directly before the current "ids_secret_name" entry:

     "value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSOwner",

   },

     "value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSSecret",
     "type": "Expression"
   },
3. Remove the incorrect parameter key "IDSOwner" entirely, as its function is now fulfilled by "Arg10".
4. Delete the superfluous parameter "GlobalEnvironment"–it has no counterpart in the DataStage definition and must not appear in the translated activity.
5. Confirm final parameter order is exactly:
   Arg1 → Arg2 → Arg8 → Arg9 → Arg10 → ids_secret_name

The Switch activities "Switch_If_1A", "Switch_If_2A", "Switch_If_2A_Continuation", "Switch_If_3A", "Switch_If_3A_Continuation", "Switch_If_4A" and "Switch_If_4A_Continuation" are located inside the "defaultActivities" branch of another Switch activity ("Switch_CountChk"). This violates the guideline that prohibits nesting Switch activities inside another Switch’s cases.activities or defaultActivities branches. All affected DataStage conditional stages (If_1A, If_2A, If_2A_Continuation, If_3A, If_3A_Continuation, If_4A, If_4A_Continuation) must correspond to root-level Switch activities in ADF. Expected: each Switch translated from these stages should reside at the root level of the pipeline’s activities array and should be linked via dependsOn to maintain execution order. Actual: they are embedded within the default branch of "Switch_CountChk", creating an invalid nested‐Switch structure and breaking compliance with nesting rules.
failures.suggested_fix:
Remediate without renaming existing activities:
1. Cut each nested Switch activity ("Switch_If_1A", "Switch_If_2A", "Switch_If_2A_Continuation", "Switch_If_3A", "Switch_If_3A_Continuation", "Switch_If_4A", "Switch_If_4A_Continuation") from the "defaultActivities" array of "Switch_CountChk".
2. Paste each Switch as an individual root-level activity in pipeline.properties.activities after "Switch_CountChk".
3. Update dependsOn for the first pasted Switch ("Switch_If_1A") so that:
   • "activity": "Any2"  (the last actual activity that should precede it)
   • "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"]
4. For every subsequent pasted Switch, set dependsOn to the immediate previous pasted Switch to preserve the original execution chain (e.g., "Switch_If_2A" depends on "Switch_If_1A", "Switch_If_2A_Continuation" depends on "Switch_If_2A", etc.).
5. Remove any empty objects left behind in the original defaultActivities list once the Switches are moved.
6. Validate the final JSON so that no Switch activity appears inside any Switch cases.activities or defaultActivities branch.
7. Re-publish the pipeline and run JSON validation to confirm the nesting rule is fully satisfied.

• The extraneous key "IDSOwner" is included even though it is not defined in the DataStage stage parameters.
• The "ids_secret_name" parameter is not placed directly after the owner parameter (Arg10); instead, it follows the extra "IDSOwner" key, breaking the required owner-secret adjacency rule.
Expected keys (order): Arg1, Arg2, Arg8, Arg9, Arg10, ids_secret_name, GlobalEnvironment.
Actual keys: Arg1, Arg2, Arg8, Arg9, Arg10, IDSOwner, ids_secret_name, GlobalEnvironment.
failures.suggested_fix:
Correct the activity "LoadB_PDX_NTWK" as follows:
1. Delete the key-value pair whose key is "IDSOwner" from the baseParameters object.
2. Move the key-value pair "ids_secret_name": {
     "value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSSecret",
     "type": "Expression"
   } so that it is immediately after "Arg10".
3. Verify the final ordered list of keys is:
   "Arg1": "B_PDX_NTWK",
   "Arg2": "REPLACE",
   "Arg8": "B_PDX_NTWK.OPTUM.dat",
   "Arg9": "Y",
   "Arg10": {"value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSOwner","type": "Expression"},
   "ids_secret_name": {"value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_IDSSecret","type": "Expression"},
   "GlobalEnvironment": {"value": "@pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_environment","type": "Expression"}
4. Save and publish the updated pipeline.

failures.explanation: The reconverge stage 'Or_6A' is represented by activity 'Or_6A_Wait'. Its dependsOn currently lists both 'If_6A' (the IfCondition) and 'FctsIdsMbrEligBalSeq' (an internal activity that executes only when the IfCondition evaluates to TRUE). When the Skip branch is taken, 'FctsIdsMbrEligBalSeq' never runs, so the dependency on its Succeeded state can never be satisfied. This violates Check-7 rule 2 and 3: reconverge activity must appear once, outside the IfCondition, and depend only on the IfCondition itself—not on branch-exclusive activities.
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify the existing activity without renaming:
1. Open activity 'Or_6A_Wait'.
2. Replace its dependsOn array so it contains exactly one element:
   [
     {
       "activity": "If_6A",
       "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"]
     }
   ]
3. Remove the entire dependsOn entry that references "FctsIdsMbrEligBalSeq".
4. Validate that no other activities reference internal branch activities in their dependsOn for this reconverge logic.

failures.explanation: The reconverge stage 'Or_6B' is translated as 'Or_6B_Wait'. Its dependsOn lists both 'If_6B' and 'FctsIdsSubEligBalSeq'. Because 'FctsIdsSubEligBalSeq' executes only when the TRUE branch of 'If_6B' runs, the dependency will never be met when the Skip branch is taken, causing a dead-lock. This contravenes Check-7 requirements that reconverge activities depend solely on the controlling IfCondition.
failures.suggested_fix:
Amend the activity as follows:
1. Edit 'Or_6B_Wait'.
2. Set dependsOn to one element:
   [
     {
       "activity": "If_6B",
       "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"]
     }
   ]
3. Delete the dependsOn entry that references "FctsIdsSubEligBalSeq".
4. Confirm pipeline debug run completes for both TRUE and FALSE branches.

failures.explanation: Activity 'Or_7B_Wait' is intended to serve as reconverge for stage 'Or_7B'. It depends on 'If_7B' and on 'AhySubRowCntSeq', an ExecutePipeline that runs only when the TRUE branch executes. If the Skip branch is taken, 'AhySubRowCntSeq' never runs, leaving 'Or_7B_Wait' in waiting state indefinitely. This setup breaches Check-7 rule that reconverge must not depend on branch-exclusive activities.
failures.suggested_fix:
Correct the configuration:

2. Replace dependsOn with exactly:
   [
     {
       "activity": "If_7B",
       "dependencyConditions": ["Succeeded"]
     }
   ]
3. Remove the dependency entry pointing to "AhySubRowCntSeq".
4. Re-publish and verify both execution paths succeed.

failures.explanation: The translation uses 'Or_7A_Wait' for reconverge of stage 'Or_7A'. dependsOn currently references both 'If_7A' and 'MbrAtchmtRowCntSeq'. Since 'MbrAtchmtRowCntSeq' is executed only in the TRUE branch of 'If_7A', the reconverge activity hangs when the Skip branch is taken. failures.suggested_fix:

The baseParameters configuration in activity "LoadB_SUBGRP" does not match the DataStage definition for stage "LoadB_SUBGRP".

  - Actual parameter key: "IDSOwner"

• Extraneous parameter:

Each listed CCondition stage should control the execution of its downstream “Run” branch activity only when the evaluated expression is TRUE. In the translated pipeline the corresponding IfCondition activities have empty ifTrueActivities / ifFalseActivities arrays, and the branch-specific DatabricksNotebook activities (e.g., "ConcatDEADrug" for If_2A, "IdsProvDEAFkey" for If_2B, "RmProvDea" for If_2C, "LoadDEA" for If_2D, "ConcatNDCDrug" for If_6A, "IdsNdcFkey" for If_6B, "RmNDC" for If_6C and "LoadNDC" for If_6D) are placed as root-level siblings that depend only on the success of the IfCondition. This configuration causes the notebook to run regardless of the evaluated branch result, violating the exclusive branch requirement. The reconverge Wait activities (Or_* nodes) are likewise positioned incorrectly, depending on both the IfCondition and the notebook rather than on the IfCondition alone when the notebook is correctly nested. Expected: • Each notebook must reside inside the parent IfCondition.typeProperties.ifTrueActivities array. • ifFalseActivities may remain empty. • No external dependency from the notebook back to the IfCondition should exist. • The respective Wait (Or_*) activity must depend only on the parent IfCondition when the notebook is nested. Actual: • ifTrueActivities / ifFalseActivities are empty. • Notebooks are external siblings depending on "Succeeded" outcome of the parent. • Wait activities depend on both the parent and the notebook.
failures.suggested_fix:
For every listed IfCondition / notebook pair apply ALL of the following steps.
1. Remove the current "dependsOn" entry that links the notebook to the IfCondition.
2. Cut the complete notebook activity JSON block.
3. Paste that notebook block inside the parent IfCondition under "typeProperties.ifTrueActivities" (array). Ensure JSON is properly comma-separated.
4. Inside the pasted notebook block delete its "dependsOn" property entirely.
5. Locate the corresponding Wait activity (Or_2A, Or_2B, Or_2C, Or_6A, Or_6B, Or_6C). In its "dependsOn" array remove the entry that references the notebook, leaving only the dependency on the parent IfCondition.
6. Confirm the IfCondition now has:
   "typeProperties": {
       "expression": { … },
       "ifTrueActivities": [ <notebook> ],
       "ifFalseActivities": []
   }
7. Validate that no other activity in the pipeline points directly to the relocated notebook. All downstream activities must point to the Wait (Or_*) or to the IfCondition as appropriate.
8. Repeat steps 1-7 independently for:
   • If_2A / ConcatDEADrug / Or_2A
   • If_2B / IdsProvDEAFkey / Or_2B
   • If_2C / RmProvDea / Or_2C
   • If_2D / LoadDEA / (next downstream activity)
   • If_6A / ConcatNDCDrug / Or_6A
   • If_6B / IdsNdcFkey / Or_6B
   • If_6C / RmNDC / Or_6C
   • If_6D / LoadNDC / (next downstream activity)
After completing these steps each notebook will execute ONLY on the TRUE branch of its parent IfCondition, and branch reconvergence will function correctly.

failures.explanation:
In the DataStage job, stage 'If_2C' is reached only through the 'Load' output link of the job activity 'BCAFEPMedClmPKExtr'. Therefore, in the translated pipeline the IfCondition activity that represents 'If_2C' must be executed strictly after the DatabricksNotebook activity 'BCAFEPMedClmPKExtr' and must reside inside the true branch (ifTrueActivities) of the enclosing IfCondition that represents 'If_2A'.  
However, the translated ADF pipeline declares the activity 'If_2C' at the root level and sets its dependsOn.activity to 'If_2A'. This means 'If_2C' will run even when 'BCAFEPMedClmPKExtr' is skipped (i.e., when the false branch of 'If_2A' executes), breaking the original branch-exclusive execution semantics. The reconverge semantics are also violated because 'If_2C' is not nested under the branch where it logically belongs.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Remove the root-level 'If_2C' activity from the top-level activities array.  
2. Insert a new 'If_2C' IfCondition activity inside the 'ifTrueActivities' array of the existing 'If_2A' activity, immediately after the DatabricksNotebook activity 'BCAFEPMedClmPKExtr'.  
3. Set the new 'If_2C'.dependsOn to reference the activity 'BCAFEPMedClmPKExtr' with dependencyConditions ["Succeeded"].  
4. Remove the previous dependency on 'If_2A'.  
5. Ensure all child activities (e.g., the DatabricksNotebook 'LoadKClm') remain unchanged but are now nested correctly under the new in-branch 'If_2C'.  
6. Verify that the downstream Wait activity 'Or_2B' depends on the repositioned 'If_2B' branch reconverge exactly once at root level.

failures.explanation:
In the original DataStage design, stage 'If_2D' is reached solely through the 'Load' link emitted by job activity 'BCAFEPMedClmLnPKExtr'. Consequently, the translated IfCondition 'If_2D' must execute only after the DatabricksNotebook 'BCAFEPMedClmLnPKExtr' succeeds and must be nested inside the true branch of 'If_2B'.  
The translated pipeline instead places 'If_2D' at the root level and sets its dependsOn.activity to 'If_2B'. This allows 'If_2D' to run even when 'BCAFEPMedClmLnPKExtr' is skipped (the false branch of 'If_2B'), violating branch exclusivity and reconverge rules.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Delete the root-level 'If_2D' activity from the activities array.

failures.explanation:
For parameter 'ErrorCount' produced by function 'UnixFileRecCnt()':
• DatabricksNotebook 'RowCountErrorClms_ErrorCount' executes the function correctly, but the subsequent SetVariable activity is mis-configured.
• SetVariable 'Set_RowCountErrorClms_ErrorCount' sets variableName to "RowCountErrorCount". The variable name must exactly match the parameter name "ErrorCount".
• A corresponding pipeline-level variable named "RowCountErrorCount" is erroneously declared while the required variable "ErrorCount" already exists. This violates the rule that the SetVariable activity must target the variable whose name equals the parameter name and that no superfluous variables are introduced.
Expected:
  – variableName: "ErrorCount"
  – No declaration of an extra variable "RowCountErrorCount" in properties.variables.
Actual:
  – variableName: "RowCountErrorCount"
  – Extraneous variable "RowCountErrorCount" declared.
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify WITHOUT renaming activities:
1. In pipeline.properties.variables:
   • Remove the entire entry for "RowCountErrorCount".
2. In activity "Set_RowCountErrorClms_ErrorCount":
   • Change "typeProperties.variableName" value from "RowCountErrorCount" to "ErrorCount".
   • Keep the existing expression for "typeProperties.value" unchanged.
3. Ensure no other activity references "RowCountErrorCount" after this change. All references must use "ErrorCount".
4. Validate that duplicate declarations do not exist; only one variable named "ErrorCount" should remain in properties.variables.

failures.explanation:
The listed DataStage stages are missing from the translated ADF pipeline:
• If_4B – a CCondition stage that determines execution of downstream activities.
• MvClmLandExtr – a CExecCommandActivity stage responsible for file movement and compression operations.
• Or_4B – a CSequencer stage that reconverges the Skip and Ok branches.
These stages are essential for faithfully reproducing the original control flow yet have no corresponding DatabricksNotebook, IfCondition, Wait, or other permitted activity types in the translated pipeline.

The activity "WriteRunID" does not pass the parameter "EntryName" exactly as required.
• Expected value: "@concat(variables('JobName'),':','RunID')" so the resolved string becomes "<JobName>:RunID" exactly matching the DataStage expression "Initial.JobName:\"RunID\"".
• Actual value observed: "@concat(variables('JobName'),'RunID')" – missing the separating colon, resulting in an incorrect string such as "<JobName>RunID".
• Structure ( {"value":..., "type":"Expression"} ) is correct, but the expression inside is wrong.
• No other parameters are missing or out of order for this activity.
failures.suggested_fix:
Update the existing activity WITHOUT renaming it.
1. Open activity "WriteRunID".
2. Navigate to typeProperties → baseParameters → EntryName.
3. Replace the current object with the following exactly:
   "EntryName": {
     "value": "@concat(variables('JobName'),':','RunID')",
     "type": "Expression"
   }
4. Ensure the key order under baseParameters remains: EntryName first, followed by GlobalEnvironment (as already present).
5. Publish the pipeline after saving the change.

failures.explanation: The DataStage CCondition stage "If_2A" has five exclusive output branches (Medtrak, SavRx, Email, LDI, CVS). In the translated ADF pipeline the activity "If_2A" is defined as an IfCondition whose expression is "@bool(true)" and both branch-lists (ifTrueActivities / ifFalseActivities) are empty. All downstream activities ("MedtrakPClmMbrErrExtr", "SavRxPClmMbrErrExtr", "Email_Failure", "LDIPClmMbrErrExtr", "CVSPClmMbrErrExtr") are placed as root-level siblings and connected only through dependsOn. This loses the original branch logic, causes every branch to execute unconditionally, and violates the requirement that each branch-specific stage must reside inside the correct branch list or a Switch case.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Delete the current root-level dependsOn connections from "MedtrakPClmMbrErrExtr", "SavRxPClmMbrErrExtr", "Email_Failure", "LDIPClmMbrErrExtr" and "CVSPClmMbrErrExtr".
2. Convert the activity "If_2A" to a Switch activity (type: "Switch").
   • keep the same name "If_2A".
   • set typeProperties.on.value to the appropriate composite expression that evaluates the five trigger predicates from the original Stage (Medtrak, SavRx, Email, LDI, CVS).
3. Create five cases under "If_2A" – "Medtrak", "SavRx", "Email", "LDI", "CVS" – each with its own activities array.
4. Move the corresponding notebook or ExecutePipeline activity into its correct case branch:
   • case "Medtrak" → activity "MedtrakPClmMbrErrExtr" (dependsOn inside branch untouched).
   • case "SavRx"   → activity "SavRxPClmMbrErrExtr".
   • case "Email"   → activity "Email_Failure".
   • case "LDI"     → activity "LDIPClmMbrErrExtr".
   • case "CVS"     → activity "CVSPClmMbrErrExtr".
5. Remove the empty ifTrueActivities / ifFalseActivities arrays from "If_2A".
6. Ensure every branch-internal activity has no external dependsOn that escapes the branch; use intra-branch dependencies only.
7. After all branches, create a single root-level Wait activity named "Any" that dependsOn the last activity inside each branch (MedtrakPClmMbrErrExtr, SavRxPClmMbrErrExtr, Email_Failure, LDIPClmMbrErrExtr, CVSPClmMbrErrExtr) with dependencyConditions: ["Succeeded"].

failures.explanation: The DataStage stage "If_2B" has two branches – "Run" (to "BCBSCommonErrClmMbrUpd") and "Skip" (to sequencer "Or_2B"). In the translation, "If_2B" is an IfCondition with expression "@bool(true)" and empty branch lists. The downstream notebook "BCBSCommonErrClmMbrUpd" and Wait activity "Or_2B" are placed as root-level siblings connected only through dependsOn, causing both paths to run regardless of the true/false outcome.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Remove dependsOn links from "BCBSCommonErrClmMbrUpd" and "Or_2B" that reference "If_2B".
2. Edit activity "If_2B" (keep type IfCondition):
   • Set typeProperties.expression to the exact translation of the original TriggerExpression for link "Run" (Count(..)=0) using Data Factory expression syntax.
3. Populate typeProperties.ifTrueActivities with the existing activity "BCBSCommonErrClmMbrUpd". Ensure its dependsOn inside the branch is empty or adjusted for intra-branch sequencing.
4. Populate typeProperties.ifFalseActivities with a single Wait activity named "If_2B_Skip_Dummy" that dependsOn nothing.
5. Change the Wait activity "Or_2B" into a root-level Wait that dependsOn both branch-terminal activities: "BCBSCommonErrClmMbrErrUpd" (Succeeded) and "If_2B_Skip_Dummy" (Succeeded), thereby re-establishing branch reconvergence.
6. Verify no other activities depend directly on "If_2B".

failures.explanation: The original DataStage stage "If_2C" contains a "Run" branch leading to "BCBSCommonPClmMbrErrMtchDel" and a "Skip" branch leading to sequencer "Or_2C". In the ADF pipeline "If_2C" is configured with expression "@bool(true)" and has empty branch arrays; the notebook "BCBSCommonPClmMbrErrMtchDel" and Wait "Or_2C" are root-level siblings connected via dependsOn. This eliminates conditional execution and misplaces branch-exclusive content.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Detach "BCBSCommonPClmMbrErrMtchDel" and "Or_2C" from direct dependsOn on "If_2C".
2. Update "If_2C" expression to the translated form of the original Run-branch predicate (Count(..)=0).
3. Insert "BCBSCommonPClmMbrErrMtchDel" into ifTrueActivities array.
4. Create a Wait activity named "If_2C_Skip_Dummy" (no dependsOn) and place it in ifFalseActivities.
5. Modify root-level Wait "Or_2C" so it depends on both "BCBSCommonPClmMbrErrMtchDel" and "If_2C_Skip_Dummy" with dependencyConditions ["Succeeded"].
6. Confirm no other activities depend directly on "If_2C".

failures.explanation: The DataStage stage "If_5B" evaluates the condition "RowCount1.ErrorCount1 > 1" with branches "Run" → "FTPErrMbr_Rx" and "Skip" → sequencer "Or_5B". The translated activity "If_5B" correctly uses the expression "@greater(int(variables('ErrorCount1')),1)" but both branch arrays are empty; "FTPErrMbr_Rx" and Wait "Or_5B" are placed outside the IfCondition. This means both branches execute after the IfCondition regardless of the predicate result.
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Remove "FTPErrMbr_Rx" and "Or_5B" dependsOn links that reference "If_5B".
2. Add "FTPErrMbr_Rx" to ifTrueActivities of "If_5B".
3. Create a Wait activity "If_5B_Skip_Dummy" (no dependsOn) and add it to ifFalseActivities.
4. Modify Wait "Or_5B" so it depends on "FTPErrMbr_Rx" and "If_5B_Skip_Dummy" with dependencyConditions ["Succeeded"].
5. Check that subsequent activity "CloseRunCycle" depends on "Or_5B" only.
6. Ensure no other activities have direct dependencies on "If_5B".

failures.explanation:
For stage 'Run_Cycle' the inbuilt function OpenRunCycle is translated into two notebook–setVariable chains, but both notebook activities contain critical configuration errors:
• Activity 'Run_Cycle_OpenRunCycle_1':
  – baseParameters.OpenRunCycle_a2, OpenRunCycle_a3, OpenRunCycle_a4 and OpenRunCycle_a5 are hard-coded literal strings ("Initial_Source","Initial_Target","Initial_Subject","Initial_Frequency"). They must reference the corresponding pipeline parameters using proper expression syntax.
  – The owner parameter OpenRunCycle_a1 passes the global parameter '$IDSOwner', but the mandatory secret parameter 'ids_secret_name' is completely missing. Owner–secret pairing is required and the secret key must be positioned immediately after the owner key.
• Activity 'Run_Cycle_OpenRunCycle_2':
  – baseParameters are correct for literal values NABP, IDS, PROVIDERS and DAILY, but the required secret parameter 'ids_secret_name' is missing right after the owner parameter OpenRunCycle_a1.
• Because of the missing secret parameter the owner-secret sequence rule is violated in both activities.

For stage 'LoopVariables' the inbuilt function GetRunCycleDate is translated into notebook activity 'LoopVariables_GetRunInfo_2', but its configuration deviates from required standards:
• baseParameters.GetRunCycleDate_a2, GetRunCycleDate_a3 and GetRunCycleDate_a4 are literal strings ("Initial_Subject","Initial_Source","Initial_Target"). They must reference pipeline parameters so that runtime values flow correctly.
• The first argument GetRunCycleDate_a1 passes the global parameter '$IDSOwner', yet the mandatory secret parameter 'ids_secret_name' is missing. The owner–secret pair must be provided with the secret key directly following the owner key.
• Parameter sequencing is therefore incorrect and violates the owner-secret immediacy rule.

For parameter 'FileList' produced by inbuilt function "LIST.FILES()":
• ERROR in activity 'ListFiles_NB' (DatabricksNotebook):
  - baseParameters.LIST.FILES_a1 is set to literal "landing".
  - Expected LIST.FILES_a1 to reference the global parameter $FilePath concatenated with the literal '/landing' using the required expression structure.
  - Expected configuration:
    "LIST.FILES_a1": {
        "value": "@concat(pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_FilePath,'/landing')",
        "type": "Expression"
    }
  - Actual configuration:
    "LIST.FILES_a1": "landing"
• The remaining parameters and the downstream SetVariable activity are correct, but the incorrect value of LIST.FILES_a1 will cause the routine to receive an incomplete path.
• Expected vs Actual summary:
  LIST.FILES_a1 – EXPECTED expression referencing gbl_param_FilePath; ACTUAL hard-coded literal "landing".
failures.suggested_fix:
Modify WITHOUT renaming activities:
1. For activity 'ListFiles_NB':
   • Open the activity’s typeProperties.baseParameters section.
   • REMOVE the existing key–value pair:
     "LIST.FILES_a1": "landing"
   • INSERT the corrected key–value pair exactly as shown below, preserving order:
     "LIST.FILES_a1": {
       "value": "@concat(pipeline().globalParameters.gbl_param_FilePath,'/landing')",
       "type": "Expression"
     }
   • Ensure the key appears before "LIST.FILES_a2" and after any owner/secret pairs if they exist.
2. No changes are required for the SetVariable activity 'Set_FileList' or variable declarations.
3. Publish the pipeline and re-test to confirm the LIST.FILES notebook now receives the full landing-directory path.

failures.explanation:
The branch generated from DataStage stage "If_2A" is not re-joined correctly in the translated ADF pipeline.
• In the source job, when the SkipDontLoad branch evaluates to TRUE, control flows: If_2A → LogMessage → Mv_Src_File.
• In the translated pipeline, activity "LogMessage" is placed inside the ifTrueActivities branch of "If_2A_SkipDontLoad" (correct), but activity "Mv_Src_File" is configured with "dependsOn" pointing to the parent IfCondition ("If_2A_SkipDontLoad") instead of the last branch-exclusive activity ("LogMessage").
• This causes "Mv_Src_File" to start concurrently with "LogMessage" rather than sequentially, breaking the intended control flow.
Expected: "Mv_Src_File" must appear once, outside the IfCondition, and depend **only** on "LogMessage" with dependencyConditions ["Succeeded"].
Actual: "Mv_Src_File" appears outside the IfCondition but depends directly on "If_2A_SkipDontLoad".
failures.suggested_fix:
1. Locate activity "Mv_Src_File" in the root-level activities list.
2. Replace its entire "dependsOn" array with:
   [
     {
       "activity": "LogMessage",
       "dependencyConditions": [
         "Succeeded"
       ]
     }
   ]
3. Remove any dependency object that references "If_2A_SkipDontLoad" from "Mv_Src_File".
4. Do not rename any activities.
5. Validate that no other activity still points to "If_2A_SkipDontLoad" when it should reference "LogMessage".
6. Publish the pipeline and confirm runtime execution order: "If_2A_SkipDontLoad" → "LogMessage" → "Mv_Src_File".

4. Ensure the expression retains the "type": "Expression" property.
5. Save the activity and publish the pipeline.

2. Replace the entire "baseParameters" section with the exact block below:
   "baseParameters": {
